title: Christ Pantocrator
highlights: (Ancient Greek: Χριστὸς Παντοκράτωρ, lit. 'Christ the Almighty') is a specific depiction of Jesus. Pantocrator or Pantokrator ( 'ruler of all'; normally translated as 'almighty' or 'all-powerful') is derived from one of many names of God in Judaism. The Pantocrator is largely an Eastern Orthodox, Eastern Catholic or Eastern  ...    The most common translation of Pantocrator is "Almighty" or "All-powerful".
